data_in.description: Cleaned organism table (interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz) containing standardized and verified organism names

data_out.description: OTL-enriched organism dictionary in SQLite format (interim/dictionaries/organism/otl.sqlite)

domain_knowledge: The Open Tree of Life (OTL) is a standardized taxonomic resource that provides stable identifiers for organisms; mapping cleaned organism names to OTL requires fuzzy matching and validation strategies to handle nomenclatural variation.
OTL identifiers serve as canonical references for downstream taxonomic and phylogenetic analyses, enabling cross-dataset organism reconciliation.
The cleaned organism table has already passed verification steps (4_cleaningTaxonomy.R) and contains verified organism records suitable for OTL mapping.
OTL enrichment produces both a SQLite dictionary for efficient lookup and potentially tabular outputs (.tsv.gz) for integration with the main curated data table.

direct_checks: verify file interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz exists in lotusnprod/lotus-processor repository
verify file 5_addingOTL.R exists in lotusnprod/lotus-processor repository
verify script 5_addingOTL.R runs without error when executed with R, using interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz as input
verify at least one output file matching pattern interim/dictionaries/organism/*.tsv is produced after script execution
verify output file(s) in interim/dictionaries/organism/ are in TSV format (tab-separated values, .tsv extension)
verify output TSV file(s) contain at least two columns: one for organism identifiers and one for Open Tree of Life identifiers, robust to column naming conventions
expert_review: assess whether Open Tree of Life identifier mappings in output dictionary are semantically correct for a representative sample of organism entries
assess whether the script appropriately handles missing or ambiguous organism names in the cleaned input table

expected_outputs: OTL-enriched organism dictionary in SQLite format (interim/dictionaries/organism/otl.sqlite)
finding: The 5_addingOTL.R script processes the cleaned organism table (interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz) to map organism names to Open Tree of Life identifiers, producing an OTL-enriched organism dictionary output in interim/dictionaries/organism/.
inputs: Cleaned organism table (interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz) containing standardized and verified organism names
methodology_summary: Load the cleaned and taxonomy-verified organism table from interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz.
Apply OTL name-to-identifier mapping using fuzzy matching and validation heuristics to assign stable taxonomic identifiers to each organism record.
Enrich organism records with OTL metadata (identifiers, taxonomic ranks, synonyms, hierarchy).
Write the enriched dictionary to interim/dictionaries/organism/otl.sqlite for indexed lookup and validation.
Validation: Verify that all records in the cleaned organism table receive OTL identifiers and that the SQLite file is syntactically correct and queryable.

workflow_description: 1. Load the cleaned organism table from interim/tables/2_cleaned/organism/cleaned.tsv.gz. 2. Execute 5_addingOTL.R to map cleaned organism names to OTL taxonomic identifiers and annotations. 3. Write the enriched organism dictionary to interim/dictionaries/organism/otl.sqlite and associated output files (.tsv.gz format).
